methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $400M last post money.

data.narrative:
AiDash is a Series C-stage infrastructure and logistics company founded by Abhishek Singh. The company has raised $91M+ to date and carries a current post-money valuation of $400M+. Valuation analysis is constrained by the absence of direct public comparables and limited availability of recent private transaction data in the infrastructure and logistics sector; accordingly, this assessment relies on stage-progression methodology rather than a multi-method triangulation.

The stage-progression approach applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier, typical for Series C to subsequent-stage advancement, to the $400M last post-money valuation. This yields an implied valuation of $680M. Given the single-method foundation, a quantitative range has been constructed around this central estimate: low case of $578M (15% discount), median of $680M, and high case of $782M (15% premium). These bounds reflect execution risk and market timing variability rather than independent valuation methods.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $578M to $782M, with $680M as the central point estimate. Key uncertainties include the absence of sector comparables to validate the 1.7x multiplier, the trajectory and timing of the company's next funding event, and market conditions for infrastructure-technology investments. Refinement would benefit from either identified peer transactions or updated guidance on unit economics and growth metrics.
